case_title: JAMES M. STEIN VS. FIRENZE FINE ARTS INC

docket_entries.description: PROOF OF SERVICE BY MAIL OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT FIRENZE FINE ARTS, INC.'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT; PROPOSED ORDER. FILED BY PLAINTIFF STEIN, JAMES M.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FIRENZE FINE ARTS, INC. MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT ENTERED ON OCTOBER 27, 2016 IS GRANTED. THE MARYLAND COURT LACKED PERSONAL JURISDICTION OVER FIRENZE. NEITHER LUBERSKI, INC. V. OLEFICIO F.LLI AMATO, S.R.L. (2009) 171 CAL. APP. 4TH 409 NOR ANY OTHER CASE HOLDS THAT A ONE-TIME MAILING OF GOODS TO AN OUT-OF-STATE ADDRESS AFTER THE GOODS WERE PURCHASED IN AN ENTIRELY IN-STATE TRANSACTION IS SUFFICIENT TO WARRANT IMPOSITION OF SPECIFIC PERSONAL JURISDICTION OVER THE MAILER BY THE STATE TO WHOM THE GOODS WERE MAILED. A RECENT UNITED STATES SUPREME COURT DECISION ON SPECIFIC JURISDICTION STRONGLY SUGGESTS THAT SPECIFIC JURISDICTION DOES NOT LIE UNDER THESE CIRCUMSTANCES. (WALDEN V. FIORE (2014) 134 S.CT. 1115.). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Richard H. Poulson, Attorney at Law, Tel. 510.747.8034, for plaintiffs;
Deborah L. Goodman, of Anderson & Poole, Tel. 415.956.6413, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT FIRENZE FINE ARTS, INC. MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT entered on October 27, 2016 is granted. The Maryland court lacked personal jurisdiction over Firenze. Neither Luberski, Inc. v. Oleficio F.LLI Amato, S.R.L. (2009) 171 Cal. App. 4th 409 nor any other case holds that a one-time mailing of goods to an out-of-state address after the goods were purchased in an entirely in-state transaction is sufficient to warrant imposition of specific personal jurisdiction over the mailer by the state to whom the goods were mailed. A recent United States Supreme Court decision on specific jurisdiction strongly suggests that specific jurisdiction does not lie under these circumstances. (Walden v. Fiore (2014) 134 S.Ct. 1115.). Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
